module.name: Web3 Development & Strategy
module.description: Comprehensive training in blockchain technology, decentralized applications, smart contracts, and cryptographic security.

web_three_blockchain_fundamentals.name: Blockchain Fundamentals
web_three_blockchain_fundamentals.description: Foundational knowledge of blockchain technology, its architecture, and core principles.

web_three_decentralized_applications.name: Decentralized Applications
web_three_decentralized_applications.description: Training in developing and integrating front-end and back-end components for dApps on Web3 infrastructure.

web_three_decentralized_identity_and_pri.name: Decentralized Identity & Privacy
web_three_decentralized_identity_and_pri.description: Exploring self-sovereign identity, zero-knowledge proofs, and privacy-enhancing blockchain solutions.

web_three_smart_contract_development.name: Smart Contract Development
web_three_smart_contract_development.description: Comprehensive training in writing, deploying, and optimizing smart contracts on blockchain networks.

web_three_tokenomics_and_defi.name: Tokenomics & DeFi
web_three_tokenomics_and_defi.description: Understanding the economics of tokenized ecosystems, decentralized finance mechanisms, and governance models.

web_three_web_three_security_and_auditin.name: Web3 Security & Auditing
web_three_web_three_security_and_auditin.description: Training in identifying vulnerabilities, auditing smart contracts, and securing Web3 applications.

web_three_blockchain_fundamentals_blockc.name: Blockchain Architecture
web_three_blockchain_fundamentals_blockc.description: Understanding the core structure and components of blockchain networks.

cards.title: Block Structure & Data Storage
cards.description: Exploring how data is stored in blocks and linked in a blockchain.
cards.bulletpoints: Block headers, Merkle trees, transaction structures
cards.summaryText: Break down a block into header, body, and Merkle tree so you know exactly how transactions link and stay tamper-evident.

cards.title: Peer-to-Peer Networks
cards.description: How blockchain nodes communicate and validate transactions.
cards.bulletpoints: Node roles, full nodes vs. light nodes, decentralized networks
cards.summaryText: Map node types and gossip protocols to see how decentralized peers relay blocks and reach agreement without central servers.

cards.title: Immutability and Data Integrity
cards.description: Ensuring data cannot be altered or removed from the blockchain.
cards.bulletpoints: Cryptographic hashing, append-only structure, cryptographic proof
cards.summaryText: Follow the hash links and append-only design that make tampering obvious and keep blockchain records provably intact.

web_three_blockchain_fundamentals_consen.name: Consensus Algorithms
web_three_blockchain_fundamentals_consen.description: Exploring different mechanisms used to validate transactions and secure blockchain networks.

cards.title: Proof of Work (PoW)
cards.description: Understanding how mining secures blockchain networks.
cards.bulletpoints: Hash puzzles, mining difficulty, energy consumption
cards.summaryText: Trace how miners race through hash puzzles, adjust difficulty, and burn energy to anchor a PoW chain's security.

cards.title: Proof of Stake (PoS)
cards.description: Exploring how validators are chosen in PoS blockchains.
cards.bulletpoints: Staking, validator selection, energy efficiency
cards.summaryText: Follow the staking flow from locked tokens to validator rotation so you see why PoS cuts energy use while keeping consensus.

cards.title: Alternative Consensus Mechanisms
cards.description: Examining newer and hybrid consensus models.
cards.bulletpoints: Delegated Proof of Stake (DPoS), Proof of Authority (PoA), Byzantine Fault Tolerance (BFT)
cards.summaryText: Compare DPoS, PoA, and BFT hybrids to learn when faster, committee-driven consensus beats classic mining.

web_three_blockchain_fundamentals_crypto.name: Cryptographic Primitives
web_three_blockchain_fundamentals_crypto.description: Understanding the cryptographic foundations that secure blockchain networks.

cards.title: Hash Functions in Blockchain
cards.description: Exploring the role of cryptographic hashing in blockchain security.
cards.bulletpoints: SHA-256, collision resistance, Merkle trees
cards.summaryText: See how SHA-256 and Merkle trees lock transactions into fingerprints that flag tampering instantly.

cards.title: Public-Key Cryptography
cards.description: Understanding asymmetric cryptography and its role in blockchain.
cards.bulletpoints: Private keys, public keys, elliptic curve cryptography (ECC)
cards.summaryText: Follow private/public key pairs and ECC math to understand how wallets prove ownership without revealing secrets.

cards.title: Digital Signatures & Authentication
cards.description: How digital signatures enable secure blockchain transactions.
cards.bulletpoints: ECDSA, transaction verification, message signing
cards.summaryText: Trace how ECDSA signatures bind transactions to keys so nodes can verify authenticity before adding blocks.

web_three_decentralized_applications_dap.name: dApp Architecture
web_three_decentralized_applications_dap.description: Understanding the core components and structure of decentralized applications.

cards.title: Introduction to dApps
cards.description: Understanding the fundamental differences between traditional apps and dApps.
cards.bulletpoints: Decentralization vs. centralization, advantages of dApps, common use cases
cards.summaryText: Compare centralized apps with dApps so you can explain why decentralization, token incentives, and censorship resistance matter.

cards.title: Decentralized Backend & Blockchain Integration
cards.description: How dApps interact with blockchain networks through smart contracts and node providers.
cards.bulletpoints: Ethereum, Polygon, BSC, RPC endpoints, node providers (Infura, Alchemy)
cards.summaryText: Sketch the path from smart contract ABI to RPC calls and managed node providers so frontends can hit multiple chains reliably.

cards.title: Decentralized Storage Solutions
cards.description: Leveraging decentralized file storage systems for dApp data persistence.
cards.bulletpoints: IPFS, Arweave, Filecoin, comparing centralized vs. decentralized storage
cards.summaryText: Evaluate IPFS, Arweave, and Filecoin trade-offs to choose decentralized storage that fits your dApp's permanence and cost needs.

web_three_decentralized_applications_ora.name: Oracles and Data Feeds
web_three_decentralized_applications_ora.description: Understanding how oracles connect off-chain data to smart contracts.

cards.title: What Are Oracles?
cards.description: Exploring the role of oracles in blockchain applications.
cards.bulletpoints: Types of oracles, decentralized vs. centralized oracles, security considerations
cards.summaryText: Survey oracle models so you can weigh centralized speed against decentralized security for your dApp.

cards.title: Integrating Chainlink Oracles
cards.description: Connecting smart contracts to real-world data through Chainlink.
cards.bulletpoints: Data feeds, price oracles, randomness functions (VRF)
cards.summaryText: Wire up Chainlink data feeds and VRF calls to stream reliable prices and randomness into your contracts.

cards.title: Building a Custom Oracle
cards.description: Developing a custom oracle for secure off-chain data retrieval.
cards.bulletpoints: API integration, webhook triggers, decentralized verification
cards.summaryText: Design a custom oracle pipeline that fetches APIs, signs responses, and resists manipulation before posting on-chain.

web_three_decentralized_applications_web.name: Web3 Frontend Development
web_three_decentralized_applications_web.description: Building responsive and interactive decentralized application front-ends.

cards.title: Web3.js & Ethers.js
cards.description: Understanding how to interact with blockchain networks from the frontend.
cards.bulletpoints: Connecting to Ethereum, sending transactions, querying smart contracts
cards.summaryText: Wire up ethers.js or web3.js to connect wallets, read contract state, and push transactions from the browser.

cards.title: Wallet Integration
cards.description: Connecting users' crypto wallets to dApps for authentication and transactions.
cards.bulletpoints: MetaMask, WalletConnect, Coinbase Wallet SDK
cards.summaryText: Implement wallet connect flows with MetaMask, WalletConnect, or Coinbase SDK so users can auth and sign safely.

cards.title: UI/UX Considerations for dApps
cards.description: Best practices for designing user-friendly and secure decentralized applications.
cards.bulletpoints: Gas fee estimation, transaction confirmations, multi-chain support
cards.summaryText: Design UX that surfaces gas costs, confirms transactions, and guides users across chains without confusion.

web_three_decentralized_identity_and_pri.name: Confidential Transactions
web_three_decentralized_identity_and_pri.description: Exploring privacy-enhancing technologies for on-chain transactions and data security.

cards.title: Ring Signatures & Monero
cards.description: Understanding how ring signatures provide transaction privacy.
cards.bulletpoints: How ring signatures work, decoy inputs, Monero's privacy features
cards.summaryText: Trace Monero's ring signatures to see how decoy inputs blur spenders while keeping the ledger balanced.

cards.title: MimbleWimble Protocol
cards.description: Exploring how MimbleWimble enhances privacy and scalability.
cards.bulletpoints: Cut-through transactions, compact blockchain history, Grin & Beam implementations
cards.summaryText: Review MimbleWimble's cut-through trick that compresses history and hides amounts without sacrificing validation.

cards.title: Stealth Addresses & Confidential Payments
cards.description: How stealth addresses improve privacy in blockchain transactions.
cards.bulletpoints: How stealth addresses work, privacy advantages, real-world use cases
cards.summaryText: Learn how stealth address schemes mint one-time destinations so observers can't link payments back to the receiver.

web_three_decentralized_identity_and_pri.name: Identity on Blockchain
web_three_decentralized_identity_and_pri.description: Exploring the use of blockchain for self-sovereign identity and decentralized identity management.

cards.title: Introduction to Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI)
cards.description: Understanding how blockchain enables individuals to control their own identity.
cards.bulletpoints: Principles of SSI, traditional identity vs. decentralized identity, real-world applications
cards.summaryText: Contrast centralized identity silos with SSI so you can describe how users own credentials and share them selectively.

cards.title: Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs)
cards.description: Exploring how DIDs provide a foundation for decentralized identity.
cards.bulletpoints: W3C DID standard, DID methods, interoperability challenges
cards.summaryText: Dig into the W3C DID spec and method registries to understand how portable, resolvable identifiers power SSI.

cards.title: Verifiable Credentials & Authentication
cards.description: Understanding how credentials can be issued, verified, and stored in a trustless system.
cards.bulletpoints: Credential issuance models, cryptographic verification, selective disclosure
cards.summaryText: Track how issuers sign verifiable credentials and how holders reveal just enough data for cryptographic verification.

web_three_decentralized_identity_and_pri.name: Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) & SNARKs
web_three_decentralized_identity_and_pri.description: Deep dive into ZK proofs, zk-SNARKs, and their application in privacy-preserving blockchain systems.

cards.title: What Are Zero-Knowledge Proofs?
cards.description: Understanding how one party can prove knowledge of a fact without revealing it.
cards.bulletpoints: Interactive vs. non-interactive ZKPs, privacy-enhancing applications, cryptographic principles
cards.summaryText: Explain how zero-knowledge protocols let provers convince verifiers without exposing the underlying secret.

cards.title: zk-SNARKs & zk-STARKs
cards.description: Exploring the different types of zero-knowledge proofs and their use cases.
cards.bulletpoints: Cryptographic setup, trusted vs. trustless computation, scaling solutions
cards.summaryText: Compare zk-SNARK trusted setups with STARK transparency to pick the right proof system for your project.

cards.title: Using ZKPs for Scalability & Privacy
cards.description: How ZKPs enhance privacy while optimizing transaction scalability.
cards.bulletpoints: ZK-Rollups, privacy-preserving smart contracts, L2 solutions
cards.summaryText: See how ZK-rollups and private smart contracts use proofs to compress transactions and hide sensitive data on L2.

web_three_smart_contract_development_gas.name: Gas Efficiency Optimization
web_three_smart_contract_development_gas.description: Techniques for reducing gas costs in smart contracts and improving execution efficiency.

cards.title: Understanding Gas Costs
cards.description: Breaking down how Ethereum's gas system works and how it impacts smart contracts.
cards.bulletpoints: Gas fees, transaction costs, block limits, gas refunds
cards.summaryText: Explain how gas fees are calculated, why block gas limits matter for deployments, and ways to reclaim gas refunds when possible.

cards.title: Storage Optimization Strategies
cards.description: Minimizing storage usage to reduce on-chain costs.
cards.bulletpoints: Storage vs. memory, packing variables, immutable variables
cards.summaryText: Compare storage and memory costs, illustrate struct packing patterns, and highlight when immutables lock values cheaply.

cards.title: Efficient Loops & Computation
cards.description: Optimizing loops and function calls to lower gas costs.
cards.bulletpoints: Minimizing loop iterations, gas-efficient function calls, avoiding redundant computations
cards.summaryText: Show techniques to cap loop work, structure functions for cheaper calls, and prune duplicate computations to trim gas.

web_three_smart_contract_development_sma.name: Smart Contract Audits
web_three_smart_contract_development_sma.description: Security analysis and best practices for preventing vulnerabilities in smart contracts.

cards.title: Understanding Reentrancy Attacks
cards.description: Exploring the most notorious vulnerability in smart contracts and how to prevent it.
cards.bulletpoints: Reentrancy bug, withdrawal patterns, checks-effects-interactions pattern
cards.summaryText: Describe the reentrancy exploit sequence, illustrate unsafe withdrawal logic, and restate the checks-effects-interactions safeguard.

cards.title: Access Control & Role Management
cards.description: Ensuring only authorized entities can execute sensitive contract functions.
cards.bulletpoints: Ownable contracts, multi-signature wallets, role-based access control
cards.summaryText: Map how Ownable patterns, multisig approvals, and role-based checks gate privileged contract operations.

cards.title: Best Practices for Secure Smart Contracts
cards.description: Writing safe and efficient Solidity code to prevent security exploits.
cards.bulletpoints: Static analysis tools, security patterns, gas-efficient security measures
cards.summaryText: Highlight key auditing tools, repeat reusable security patterns, and balance safety tweaks with gas-aware tradeoffs.

web_three_smart_contract_development_sol.name: Solidity Basics
web_three_smart_contract_development_sol.description: Introduction to Solidity programming for writing smart contracts on Ethereum and EVM-compatible blockchains.

cards.title: Introduction to Solidity
cards.description: Understanding Solidity's role in blockchain development and its fundamental syntax.
cards.bulletpoints: Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM), Solidity syntax, contract deployment
cards.summaryText: Introduce how Solidity targets the EVM, review core syntax conventions, and outline the deployment flow for a basic contract.

cards.title: Solidity Data Types & Variables
cards.description: Exploring Solidity's primitive and complex data types.
cards.bulletpoints: uint, int, string, bytes, arrays, mappings, structs
cards.summaryText: List key primitive and reference types, explain when to use arrays versus mappings, and show how structs bundle related fields.

cards.title: Functions, Modifiers, and Events
cards.description: Learning how to define functions, use modifiers, and trigger events.
cards.bulletpoints: Function visibility, function modifiers, event logging
cards.summaryText: Clarify visibility keywords, demonstrate modifiers enforcing rules, and connect event emissions to off-chain listeners.

web_three_tokenomics_and_defi_defi_proto.name: DeFi Protocol Mechanics
web_three_tokenomics_and_defi_defi_proto.description: Examining the foundational mechanisms that power decentralized finance applications.

cards.title: Lending & Borrowing in DeFi
cards.description: Understanding decentralized lending platforms and borrowing strategies.
cards.bulletpoints: Collateralization, overcollateralized loans, liquidation risks
cards.summaryText: Break down collateral ratios, explain why loans stay overcollateralized, and walk through liquidation triggers on major lending protocols.

cards.title: Automated Market Makers (AMMs)
cards.description: Exploring how AMMs enable decentralized trading without order books.
cards.bulletpoints: Liquidity pools, constant product formula, impermanent loss
cards.summaryText: Describe how liquidity pools quote prices via the constant product curve and evaluate impermanent loss trade-offs for LPs.

cards.title: Yield Aggregation & Optimization
cards.description: Strategies for maximizing returns on decentralized finance investments.
cards.bulletpoints: Yield farming, staking rewards, auto-compounders, Yearn Finance
cards.summaryText: Summarize how farmers chase rewards, how auto-compounders rotate strategies, and what Yearn-style vaults automate for users.

web_three_tokenomics_and_defi_governance.name: Governance & DAOs
web_three_tokenomics_and_defi_governance.description: Deep dive into decentralized governance structures and the role of DAOs in blockchain networks.

cards.title: DAO Governance Models
cards.description: Understanding different models of decentralized autonomous organizations.
cards.bulletpoints: On-chain vs. off-chain governance, treasury control, member incentives
cards.summaryText: Contrast on-chain and off-chain coordination, outline how treasuries are safeguarded, and note incentive designs that keep contributors engaged.

cards.title: Voting Mechanisms & Consensus
cards.description: Exploring different voting mechanisms in decentralized governance.
cards.bulletpoints: Token-based voting, quadratic voting, snapshot governance
cards.summaryText: Review token-weighted voting pitfalls, show how quadratic voting balances whales, and mention Snapshot for gasless signaling.

cards.title: Protocol Treasury Management
cards.description: Managing decentralized treasuries and funding ecosystem development.
cards.bulletpoints: Treasury allocation, grants programs, inflation control
cards.summaryText: Detail treasury allocation frameworks, grant review loops, and monetary levers that keep inflation aligned with growth.

web_three_tokenomics_and_defi_token_desi.name: Token Design & Distribution
web_three_tokenomics_and_defi_token_desi.description: Fundamentals of token creation, utility models, and distribution strategies in blockchain ecosystems.

cards.title: Token Standards & Types
cards.description: Understanding different token standards and their applications.
cards.bulletpoints: ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155, fungible vs. non-fungible tokens
cards.summaryText: Compare ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 capabilities while clarifying when fungible versus non-fungible tokens best serve a product vision.

cards.title: Token Utility & Governance
cards.description: Exploring how tokens function within ecosystems and enable governance.
cards.bulletpoints: Utility tokens, governance voting, staking incentives, security tokens
cards.summaryText: Explain how utility tokens power participation, how staking and voting reinforce governance, and when regulatory constraints push designs toward security tokens.

cards.title: Token Distribution Models
cards.description: Analyzing methods for fair and strategic token distribution.
cards.bulletpoints: Airdrops, ICOs, IDOs, liquidity mining, vesting schedules
cards.summaryText: Outline pros and cons of airdrops, sales, and liquidity mining while stressing vesting schedules that guard against dumping.

web_three_web_three_security_and_auditin.name: Blockchain Penetration Testing
web_three_web_three_security_and_auditin.description: Testing the security of blockchain networks and Web3 applications.

cards.title: Smart Contract Attack Simulation
cards.description: Using attack frameworks to simulate real-world exploits.
cards.bulletpoints: Echidna, MythX, Slither, Hardhat tests
cards.summaryText: Demonstrate how Echidna, MythX, and Slither surface exploit paths and how Hardhat test harnesses reproduce them safely.

cards.title: Blockchain Network Vulnerability Assessments
cards.description: Analyzing consensus mechanisms and network security risks.
cards.bulletpoints: Sybil attacks, 51% attacks, DDoS threats on nodes
cards.summaryText: Explain how Sybil and 51% attacks emerge from weak consensus assumptions and outline node-hardening steps against DDoS.

cards.title: Web3 dApp Security Testing
cards.description: Identifying vulnerabilities in Web3 frontends and smart contract interactions.
cards.bulletpoints: Metamask phishing, API abuse, RPC endpoint security
cards.summaryText: Highlight phishing entry points in wallet flows, probe API and RPC misuse, and align fixes with least-privilege design.

web_three_web_three_security_and_auditin.name: Common Smart Contract Vulnerabilities
web_three_web_three_security_and_auditin.description: Understanding the most critical security risks in smart contract development.

cards.title: Reentrancy Attacks
cards.description: Exploiting contract execution order to drain funds from a contract.
cards.bulletpoints: Fallback functions, attack vectors, best practices for mitigation
cards.summaryText: Trace a classic reentrancy exploit step-by-step, emphasize vulnerable fallback patterns, and reiterate mitigation using CEI and reentrancy guards.

cards.title: Integer Overflow & Underflow
cards.description: Bypassing numerical limits to manipulate contract state.
cards.bulletpoints: SafeMath libraries, Solidity 0.8.x automatic checks, attack examples
cards.summaryText: Show how unchecked math corrupts balances, note Solidity 0.8.x's built-in checks, and reference SafeMath for legacy codebases.

cards.title: Unauthorized Access & Logic Flaws
cards.description: Exploiting contract functions due to improper access control.
cards.bulletpoints: Role-based permissions, function visibility, privilege escalation
cards.summaryText: Identify common access lapses, walk through privilege escalation case studies, and reinforce visibility plus role-management best practices.

web_three_web_three_security_and_auditin.name: Web3 Security Frameworks
web_three_web_three_security_and_auditin.description: Establishing best practices and security protocols for decentralized applications.

cards.title: Secure Smart Contract Development
cards.description: Implementing best practices to reduce security risks in Solidity.
cards.bulletpoints: Code audits, linting, security-focused contract patterns
cards.summaryText: Reinforce disciplined code reviews, automated linting, and battle-tested security patterns to harden Solidity deliverables.

cards.title: Web3 Security Incident Response
cards.description: Handling security breaches and contract vulnerabilities.
cards.bulletpoints: Bug bounty programs, on-chain monitoring, emergency pause mechanisms
cards.summaryText: Outline playbooks that combine bug bounties, real-time monitoring, and pause switches to contain on-chain incidents.

cards.title: Zero-Trust & Decentralized Security
cards.description: Implementing decentralized security principles in Web3 architecture.
cards.bulletpoints: Multisig wallets, hardware security modules, decentralized key management
cards.summaryText: Connect zero-trust philosophy with multisig custody, HSM usage, and resilient decentralized key management flows.
